opp.name: Careem_S2C_2026
opp.next_step: Confidence 10% 5th May - High-level quick demo provided on their priority areas iSupplier/iSource (touched on Intake& ANA), received well (only gap I can see at this point is that they want to advertise their opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients) - next step is to brief HOP and agree a way forward, have offered to provide a summary/pitch/demo to HOP - ball is in thier court, chase if nothing heard in a week or two. 29th April - Positive discovery call (SIR below), Next Steps: a) A live demo focussing on a high-level overview of the full Source-to-Pay suite (though initial focus is likely to be Sourcing & Supplier Management, with perhaps Intake Management to step change the user community experience, with CLM & Performance/Risk Management as a later phase.). b) Option to support the business case/ROI if required? - we will discuss after the demo session. SITUATION • Careem is newly rolling out Oracle eSourcing; adoption is low and they are simultaneously trying to digitize historically manual/email‑based quote collections and sourcing. • The Oracle interface is too complex for non‑tech sourcing managers and for suppliers, so they are exploring other options. • The project is owned by the controllership/procurement excellence team, who are also pushing broader automation and agentic AI across Oracle processes. IMPACT • There is “double pressure”: they must both digitize manual processes and make the new digital tool actually usable, otherwise adoption and digitization targets will fail. • Supplier onboarding and training on complex tools is viewed as the biggest challenge; if the UI isn’t intuitive, supplier participation in eSourcing events will suffer. • Usability and adoption of the live Oracle tool are already “challenged,” and they have a hard deadline not to exit 2026 without full digitization in this area. PROPOSED RESOLUTION • Zycus is a UX‑friendly, AI‑driven S2P layer that complements Oracle: with much easier eSourcing, Supplier Management/Onboarding, and guided Intake - while Oracle stays the ERP system of record. • Current thinking: start with an eSourcing & Autonomous Negotiation‑focused deployment, plus likely supplier management and Intake for step changing user experience/interface. Then phase in CLM and supplier risk/performance in later phases, aligned to their budget and digitization roadmap.

metrics.narrative: Careem articulated operational pain but did not quantify financial impact. The business problem is twofold: (1) Oracle eSourcing UI is too complex for non-tech sourcing managers and suppliers, driving low adoption; (2) procurement is largely manual and email-based, with no systematic digitization. The buyer stated there is double pressure to both digitize manual processes AND make the new digital tool actually usable, otherwise adoption and digitization targets will fail (Next_Step IMPACT section, 5 May 2026). Quantifying the value case and tying it to budget approval is the immediate move to advance this deal.

Salesforce Competitor field: Oracle

competition.narrative: Oracle eSourcing is the incumbent competitor. Careem is mid-rollout of Oracle eSourcing, but adoption is low and the UI is too complex for sourcing managers and suppliers (Next_Step SITUATION 5 May, MEDDPICC CRM Competition 4 Jun). The buyer is exploring whether to replace Oracle eSourcing with Zycus or persevere with Oracle training and extensions. Internal build is the primary competitive alternative: Careem has strong tech capability and may choose to build a custom interface on top of their existing Oracle ERP backend rather than purchasing Zycus (MEDDPICC CRM Competition and Blockers, 4 Jun). Jaggaer was referenced post-demo as a feature benchmark (advertise opportunities to market as Jaggaer does with public sector clients, Next_Step 5 May), but is not confirmed as an active competitor in this evaluation. The competitive wedge is that Zycus offers a user-friendly, supplier-friendly, AI-native platform that complements Oracle (Oracle remains ERP system of record) rather than replacing it, with proven Oracle integration via iSaaS model, and a strong local reference (Bolt, ride-hailing competitor in UAE, is an existing Zycus customer per MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). However, the buyer has not acted in 55 days, which may signal that Oracle training or internal build is winning by default. The immediate move is to address the market advertising gap (Jaggaer feature) and demonstrate that Zycus + Oracle is lower-risk and faster to value than internal build or Oracle-only perseverance, and to position the Bolt reference as proof.

sources: Next_Step__c SITUATION and IMPACT sections, updated 5 May 2026

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly identified and articulated by the buyer. The specific operational pain is that Oracle eSourcing, which Careem is newly rolling out, is too complex for non-tech sourcing managers and for suppliers, driving low adoption. Procurement is largely manual and email-based, with no systematic digitization. The Oracle interface is designed for admin or tech users, not end buyers or vendors, so supplier onboarding and training are the biggest challenges. The buyer stated there is double pressure: they must both digitize manual processes AND make the new digital tool actually usable, otherwise adoption and digitization targets will fail (Next_Step IMPACT section, 5 May 2026). Usability and adoption of the live Oracle tool are already challenged, and they have a hard deadline not to exit 2026 without full digitization in this area (Next_Step IMPACT 5 May). Additional pain points noted in MEDDPICC CRM (4 Jun) include decentralized spend across markets (thin margins, gig workforce, scale across multiple geographies creates significant indirect and tail spend complexity) and contract management risk (multi-jurisdiction, multiple business units with contracts complexity). The pain owner is the controllership and procurement excellence team, led by Zermeena Khan (Controllership) and Kashan Ahmed (Procurement Manager, Excellence Team), who are pushing broader automation and agentic AI across Oracle processes (Next_Step SITUATION 5 May, MEDDPICC CRM Champion 4 Jun). Pain is confirmed, recent, and tied to a forcing function (end-2026 deadline).

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is clear and was articulated by Kashan on the discovery call (28 Apr 2026). The steps are: (1) Procurement team (Kashan, Zermeena, and team) evaluates the solution; (2) Business case is built, with or without vendor support; (3) Budget is pitched to the CFO or approving authority; (4) Budget is approved; (5) Vendor is selected; (6) Implementation begins (MEDDPICC CRM Decision Process, 4 Jun). The buyer stated a stretch goal to be live by June 2026 (already passed) and a hard commitment to complete digitization by end-2026 (Next_Step IMPACT 5 May, MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). However, the next gate is internal: the procurement team must brief the Head of Procurement and agree a way forward before advancing to the business case and CFO pitch (Next_Step 5 May). This HOP gate is currently blocking progress: 55 days have elapsed since the demo (5 May to 29 Jun today) and the HOP has not been briefed. If the HOP is not a sponsor or is risk-averse, the deal will stall indefinitely. The path to the HOP and the HOP's level of support are unknown, and this is the immediate blocker to resolve.

sources: MEDDPICC CRM Decision Criteria, last updated 4 Jun 2026
Discovery call 28 Apr 2026 (Avoma notes, user-friendly UI, Oracle/Slack integration, AI alignment)
Next_Step__c IMPACT and gap note, 5 May 2026
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are well-articulated from the discovery call (28 Apr 2026) and confirmed in MEDDPICC CRM (4 Jun). The primary driver is a user-friendly UI for both sourcing managers and suppliers, as Oracle eSourcing is too complex and adoption is suffering (discovery call 28 Apr, MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). Second, Oracle integration is critical: Careem needs a clear integration path, API complexity assessment, and timeline, as Oracle will remain the ERP system of record (MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun, Next_Step PROPOSED RESOLUTION 5 May). Third, Slack integration is required: Careem is a Slack-only company (not Teams or Zoom), and the buyer noted on the discovery call that Merlin Intake is only available in Teams today, with Slack/Zoom/Google coming soon but not yet live (MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun, Avoma notes 28 Apr). Fourth, AI and agentic capability are important, as Careem is building internal agentic AI workflows and sees this as aligned to their roadmap (MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). Fifth, cost-efficiency and ROI must be demonstrated versus an internal build or Oracle extension (MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). Sixth, the solution must enable end-to-end digitization by year-end 2026, the hard deadline stated by the buyer (Next_Step IMPACT 5 May, MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). One additional criterion surfaced post-demo: the ability to advertise opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients (Next_Step 5 May). This gap is unaddressed and could trigger competitive exploration if material to the buyer.

north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery and demo complete, but buyer has not briefed their Head of Procurement or responded in 55 days (since 5 May 2026 demo). Close date 31 Dec 2026 leaves 185 days, but buyer stated hard deadline to complete digitization by end-2026 yet has not acted. Without re-engagement this week and HOP validation within 30 days, deal will stall indefinitely.

flags: AI differentiation under-activated. Buyer controllership/procurement excellence team is pushing agentic AI across Oracle processes (Next_Step__c), ANA (Autonomous Negotiation) is in scope, but no AI/Merlin positioning logged in demo or follow-up. This is the single highest-impact wedge vs Oracle complexity and should be the centerpiece of HOP briefing. Move: lead HOP pitch with AI/agentic negotiation use case (e.g. supplier price negotiation automation, AI-driven sourcing recommendations) and tie to buyer digitization + AI mandate.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers: (1) Re-engage Kashan this week and escalate to Zermeena to unblock HOP gate; 55 days of silence post-demo signals internal blocker (HOP not sponsor, budget not secured, or competing priority). (2) Map Economic Buyer (Ashish Jain SVP Finance or CFO) into next conversation; without EB validation of business case and budget, deal cannot progress past evaluation. (3) Position Bolt (UAE ride-hailing competitor, existing Zycus customer) as live proof of Oracle integration and regional deployment success, and co-build quantified ROI tied to Careem's end-2026 digitization deadline to convert evaluation into budget approval.

competitive_position.summary: Oracle eSourcing is the incumbent and the primary threat. Careem is mid-rollout but adoption is low due to UI complexity (Next_Step 5 May 2026). The buyer may choose to persevere with Oracle training and extensions rather than onboard Zycus, despite stated usability pain. Internal build is the second threat: Careem has strong tech capability and may build a custom interface on top of Oracle ERP rather than purchase Zycus (MEDDPICC CRM 4 Jun). Jaggaer was referenced post-demo as a feature benchmark (market advertising capability) but is not confirmed as an active competitor. The current strongest threat is inertia and internal build: 55 days of buyer inaction post-demo (5 May to 29 Jun today) suggests Oracle-only or internal build may be winning by default. Zycus wedge is user-friendly UI, supplier-friendly portal, AI-native platform (Merlin, agentic AI aligns with Careem's AI-first roadmap), proven Oracle integration via iSaaS, and strong local reference (Bolt in UAE). However, the wedge is not activating the buyer; re-engagement and HOP validation are required to move this deal.

hard.next_step:
Confidence 10%

5th May - High-level quick demo provided on their priority areas iSupplier/iSource (touched on Intake& ANA), received well (only gap I can see at this point is that they want to advertise their opportunities to the market, as Jaggaer does with public sector clients) - next step is to brief HOP and agree a way forward, have offered to provide a summary/pitch/demo to HOP - ball is in thier court, chase if nothing heard in a week or two.

29th April - Positive discovery call (SIR below), Next Steps: a) A live demo focussing on a high-level overview of the full Source-to-Pay suite (though initial focus is likely to be Sourcing & Supplier Management, with perhaps Intake Management to step change the user community experience, with CLM & Performance/Risk Management as a later phase.).

b) Option to support the business case/ROI if required? - we will discuss after the demo session.

SITUATION

•Careem is newly rolling out Oracle eSourcing; adoption is low and they are simultaneously trying to digitize historically manual/email‑based quote collections and sourcing.

•The Oracle interface is too complex for non‑tech sourcing managers and for suppliers, so they are exploring other options.

•The project is owned by the controllership/procurement excellence team, who are also pushing broader automation and agentic AI across Oracle processes.

IMPACT

•There is “double pressure”: they must both digitize manual processes and make the new digital tool actually usable, otherwise adoption and digitization targets will fail.

•Supplier onboarding and training on complex tools is viewed as the biggest challenge; if the UI isn’t intuitive, supplier participation in eSourcing events will suffer.

•Usability and adoption of the live Oracle tool are already “challenged,” and they have a hard deadline not to exit 2026 without full digitization in this area.

PROPOSED RESOLUTION

•Zycus is a UX‑friendly, AI‑driven S2P layer that complements Oracle: with much easier eSourcing, Supplier Management/Onboarding, and guided Intake - while Oracle stays the ERP system of record. 

•Current thinking: start with an eSourcing & Autonomous Negotiation‑focused deployment, plus likely supplier management and Intake for step changing user experience/interface. Then phase in CLM and supplier risk/performance in later phases, aligned to their budget and digitization roadmap.

b) Option to support the business case/ROI if required? - we will discuss after the demo session.

SITUATION

•Careem is newly rolling out Oracle eSourcing; adoption is low and they are simultaneously trying to digitize historically manual/email‑based quote collections and sourcing.

•The Oracle interface is too complex for non‑tech sourcing managers and for suppliers, so they are exploring other options.

•The project is owned by the controllership/procurement excellence team, who are also pushing broader automation and agentic AI across Oracle processes.

IMPACT

•There is “double pressure”: they must both digitize manual processes and make the new digital tool actually usable, otherwise adoption and digitization targets will fail.

•Supplier onboarding and training on complex tools is viewed as the biggest challenge; if the UI isn’t intuitive, supplier participation in eSourcing events will suffer.

•Usability and adoption of the live Oracle tool are already “challenged,” and they have a hard deadline not to exit 2026 without full digitization in this area.

PROPOSED RESOLUTION

•Zycus is a UX‑friendly, AI‑driven S2P layer that complements Oracle: with much easier eSourcing, Supplier Management/Onboarding, and guided Intake - while Oracle stays the ERP system of record.\xa0

•Current thinking: start with an eSourcing & Autonomous Negotiation‑focused deployment, plus likely supplier management and Intake for step changing user experience/interface. Then phase in CLM and supplier risk/performance in later phases, aligned to their budget and digitization roadmap.
